scenes.title: Scene 1: System Initialization
scenes.goal: Introduce and visualize the physical objects and baseline state of the system.
scenes.narration: We begin with a flat, frictionless floor. On top of it sits a large board of mass 4 kg. Two smaller blocks, Block 1 and Block 2, each with a mass of 1 kg, are placed spaced apart on top of the board.

scenes.title: Scene 2: Application of Force and Friction Limits
scenes.goal: Display the contact forces and expose the critical threshold where relative slipping begins.
scenes.narration: A horizontal force F is applied directly to the board, pulling it to the right. Static friction couples the blocks to the board, pulling them forward. Because the static friction threshold for Block 2 is lower, it will reach its breaking point and slip before Block 1 does.

scenes.title: Scene 3: Threshold Calculation Matrix
scenes.goal: Isolate the free-body dynamics to define the specific boundary conditions for p and q.
scenes.narration: By evaluating the system parameters, we mathematically define the minimum force F_min required to initiate any slipping, and the maximum force F_max before total structural breakaway occurs. These values yield our variables p and q to resolve the required parameter summation.
